---
title: "Graph analytics of stock market"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
library(duckdb)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyquant)

conn <- dbConnect(duckdb(), "data/sp500_prices_2026-04-01.duckdb", read_only = TRUE)
dbGetQuery(conn, "FROM prices LIMIT 3")
# dbGetQuery(conn, "select count(*) from prices;")
```

```{r}
df_returns <- tbl(conn, "prices") |>
  as_tibble() |>
  group_by(symbol) |>
  tq_transmute(
    select = adjusted,
    mutate_fun = periodReturn,
    period = "daily",
    type = "log",
    leading = FALSE
  ) |>
  drop_na()

df_returns |> slice_head(n = 2)
```

```{r}
df_returns |> count() |> filter(n < 812)
```


```{r}
symbol_pairs <- df_returns |> select(symbol) |> distinct() |> pull()
symbol_pairs <- expand_grid(symbol1 = symbol_pairs, symbol2 = symbol_pairs) |>
  filter(symbol1 < symbol2) # |>
  # slice_head(n = 3)
# symbol_pairs
returns12 <- symbol_pairs |>
  left_join(df_returns, join_by(symbol1 == symbol)) |>
  rename(return1 = daily.returns) |>
  left_join(df_returns, join_by(symbol2 == symbol, date == date)) |>
  rename(return2 = daily.returns)

returns12 |> slice_head(n = 5)
```
## Aggregate

```{r}
cor_return12 <- function(x, y) {
  return(cor(x, y))
}

returns12 |>
  group_by(symbol1, symbol2) |>
  summarise(correlation = cor_return12(return1, return2))
```
